rejection_reason: NON_CANONICAL_EVIDENCE_OBJECT — MPEG-4 fixture cannot close Hermes H.264 runtime-state gap

clip.note: Canonical Hermes clip (hermes-0000-1000.mp4, sha256 dd9da344...) not available on this workstation. Probe used test fixture instead.

observation-01.observation_notes: is_hwaccel=True but hw_config_device_type=null and hw_config_format=null. MPEG-4 codec has no CUDA hardware decoder, so hwaccel.config is None despite is_hwaccel=True. This means PyAV's is_hwaccel reflects 'HWAccel object was passed to av.open()' rather than 'hardware decode is active'. For H.264 (canonical Hermes clip), CUDA H.264 decoder may produce non-null config, but cannot verify without the clip.

strict_fallback_control.note: Strict probe (allow_software_fallback=False) with CUDA HWAccel on MPEG-4 codec succeeded. This does not prove actual hardware decode — MPEG-4 has no CUDA decoder, so FFmpeg likely allocates software surfaces despite the strict setting.

conclusions.runtime_active_state_interpretation: stream.codec_context.is_hwaccel=True confirms HWAccel object was passed to av.open(). hw_config_device_type=null and hw_config_format=null indicate no actual hardware decoder configuration was established. For MPEG-4 (no CUVA decoder), this implies software decode despite is_hwaccel=True.
conclusions.fallback_state_note: PyAV does not directly expose a software-fallback-detected flag. hw_config=null is the closest indirect evidence of fallback for this codec.
conclusions.evidence_codec_limitation: Probe used MPEG-4 test fixture, not the canonical H.264 Hermes clip. H.264 with CUDA may produce non-null hw_config. This evidence confirms the observability mechanism works and the production path requests CUDA, but cannot confirm actual H.264 hardware decode activation without the canonical clip.
limitations: Canonical Hermes clip (H.264) not available — probe used MPEG-4 test fixture
is_hwaccel=True with config=null suggests HWAccel passed but HW decoder not active for this codec
H.264 CUDA decode may produce different config state — cannot verify
Strict fallback probe succeeded but may not prevent software fallback for unsupported codecs
